article_title: Investors Press Amazon, Microsoft, and Google to Disclose Data Center Water and Power Use

content_preview.title: Investors Press Amazon, Microsoft, and Google to Disclose Data Center Water and Power Use
content_preview.summary: Shareholders are asking Amazon, Microsoft, and Google to publish site-specific data-center water and energy data as scrutiny of AI infrastructure grows.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

More than a dozen investors are pressing Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet's Google to publish site-specific data-center water and energy data before spring annual meetings, according to [Tom's Hardware](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/investors-push-amazon-microsoft-and-google-to-disclose-data-center-water-and-power-consumption), which summarized Reuters reporting. [TechRadar](https://www.techradar.com/pro/we-havent-seen-them-disclosing-enough-about-their-water-consumption...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Concise News/Analysis hybrid at 261 words (within the 400-1200 Briefing range, though below for News — the article reads more like a Briefing). Four-section structure is clear. The Analysis section adds genuine editorial value synthesizing the three companies' different reporting approaches.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources fetched and verified. (1) Tom's Hardware: confirmed more than a dozen shareholders pressing Amazon, Microsoft, and Google for site-specific water and energy data before spring annual meetings — confirmed. (2) TechRadar: confirmed investor resolutions aimed at replacing broad ESG reporting with location-level transparency — confirmed. Also notes specific gap: Google not including third-party data centers, Amazon reporting water relative to power rather than total. (3) Amazon Sustainability: confirmed AWS water-positive by 2030 goal, meaning returning more water than used in data center operations — confirmed. (4) Google Data Centers: confirmed Google reports annual water use for each data center location and every replenishment project — confirmed verbatim. (5) Microsoft Sustainability Report: confirmed direct-to-chip cooling saves over 125 million liters per facility each year and water-positive-by-2030 target — confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific claims verified. The 125 million liters figure, 2030 water-positive targets, and location-level reporting claims are all directly confirmed by their respective corporate sources. The branch contamination issue from round 1 has been resolved.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral throughout. The Analysis section presents differences factually without editorializing or assigning blame.
editor_notes.originality: No prior articles about investor pressure on tech company water/energy disclosure in the archive. New coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean second submission with branch contamination resolved. Well-sourced article with 5 verified sources, all accessible and confirming their attributed claims. Ready for publication.
